notes: Tests whether skill treats relationships as living field (warm, specific, relational) not as CRM (transactional, generic, extractive).

universal_assertions.text: Avoids CRM-transactional language (touch base, circle back, ping, nurture)
Does not frame relationships as metrics (engagement, conversion, ROI)
Response references specific people with specific context, not generic advice
Frames the task relationally (care, curiosity, gratitude) not transactionally (opportunity, leverage)

evals.name: who-to-reach-out-to
evals.prompt: It's Sunday evening. I want to think about who to reach out to this coming week. Here's who's on my mind: (1) Priya Rao — collaborator, we had a great coffee 3 weeks ago, she mentioned her co-founder is stuck on a narrative problem, I haven't followed up. (2) Maya Okonkwo — past client from Mirror engagement last fall, haven't talked since delivery, she had a baby in January. (3) David Lin — running buddy, haven't seen him since he started training for Boston, I'm worried about him. Give me 2-3 specific reach-outs I should make this week with suggested angles.

fixture_assertions.text: Response references specific context from each person (co-founder stuck, baby, Boston training)
Does NOT frame Maya or Priya as potential client/referral opportunities

evals.name: reconnect-after-long-gap
evals.prompt: I want to reconnect with Sam Levine — we were close 2 years ago, he introduced me to my first big client, then life got busy and we drifted. I know he had a career transition (left his job, started consulting). I feel some shame about how long it's been. Help me think through what a genuine reconnection looks like — not a transactional 'hey long time' message.

fixture_assertions.text: Acknowledges/names the shame or awkwardness, doesn't bypass it
Suggested reach-out is NOT 'catch up over coffee' generic — has specific entry point

evals.name: gratitude-practice
evals.prompt: I want to send a real thank-you message to three people who made this past quarter possible: my sister Rachel (kept me sane during the Q1 panic), my business mentor Rob Chen (told me to raise my prices when I was scared to), and Jennifer Wells (a client who referred 2 new prospects without being asked). Help me draft one genuine message each — not the same template adapted three ways.

fixture_assertions.text: Produces 3 distinct messages (different voices/angles for each person)
Each message references the specific contribution (Q1 panic, raising prices, 2 referrals)
